question: I'm preparing a DARPA Phase II proposal. Compare SBIR vs STTR work-share if I use a university and an FFRDC; what must I disclose if I also submit a similar proposal elsewhere; and for an OT, what must the Milestone Plan and Commercialization Strategy include, including optional letters?
expected_answer: SBIR requires the proposer to perform at least one-half/50% of Phase II work; STTR requires at least 40% by the proposer and 30% by the RI. FFRDCs are allowed without waiver but must be certified on the Cover Sheet; other federal orgs are not permitted; funding cannot go directly to the lab/FFRDC. Similar proposals may be submitted but essentially equivalent awards are unlawful and must be disclosed before award. OT requires a Milestone Plan with description, exit criteria, due date, payment schedule, and Government data rights; Commercialization Strategy goes at the end of Volume 2, max 5 pages, not counting against the page limit; optional Advocacy Letters and Letters of Intent/Commitment do not count against page limits.

question: If my SBIR Phase II team includes a university and an FFRDC, what work-share minimum applies to me, what Cover Sheet action is required for the FFRDC, and can the Agency pay the FFRDC directly?
expected_answer: The proposer must perform at least one-half of the research/analytical work. FFRDC use requires Cover Sheet certification but no waiver. The Agency cannot send SBIR/STTR funding directly to the Federal Laboratory or FFRDC; the SBC must contract with them.

question: For STTR Phase II, what minimum percentages apply to the proposer and the Research Institution, and are FFRDC subcontracts allowed?
expected_answer: At least 40% by the proposer and at least 30% by the RI. FFRDC/Federal Laboratory subcontracts are allowed without a waiver if certified on the Cover Sheet.

question: Can I submit essentially the same proposal to two agencies, and separately, what five fields must each OT milestone include?
expected_answer: Submitting essentially equivalent proposals is permissible, but awards requiring essentially equivalent effort are unlawful and any question must be disclosed before award. Each OT milestone must include description, completion/exit criteria, due date, payment/funding schedule, and Government data rights for each data deliverable.

question: For an OT Phase II proposal, where does the Transition and Commercialization Strategy go, what is its page limit, and which optional letters may support it?
expected_answer: It must be included at the end of the Technical Volume (Volume 2), must not exceed 5 pages, and does not count against the proposal page limit. Optional Advocacy Letters and Letters of Intent/Commitment may support transition/commercialization claims and also do not count against page limits.

question: Distinguish the proposal Transition and Commercialization Strategy requirements from optional supporting letters, including page-count treatment.
expected_answer: The Strategy is required at the end of Volume 2, max 5 pages, and does not count against the Technical Volume page limit. Advocacy Letters and Letters of Intent/Commitment are optional, do not count against page limits, and should only substantiate commercialization claims.

question: An STTR proposer using an FFRDC also wants an OT. What STTR work-share rules apply and what mandatory OT planning document is required?
expected_answer: STTR requires at least 40% work by the proposer and 30% by the RI; FFRDC use needs Cover Sheet certification. An OT requires a detailed Milestone Plan.

question: What is the Technical Volume page limit, and does the Transition and Commercialization Strategy count against that limit?
expected_answer: The Technical Volume has a defined page limit (commonly 20 pages in the instructions). The Transition and Commercialization Strategy should not exceed 5 pages and will NOT count against the proposal page limit.

question: If I plan both an FFRDC subcontract under SBIR and simultaneous similar proposals to another agency, what certification and disclosure rules apply?
expected_answer: FFRDC use must be certified on the Cover Sheet (no waiver). Similar/identical proposals may be submitted, but essentially equivalent effort awards are unlawful and any question must be disclosed before award.

question: List the five required OT milestone fields and state whether proprietary data should appear in the OT package.
expected_answer: Required fields: milestone description; completion/exit criteria; due date; payment/funding schedule; Government data rights for each data deliverable. Proprietary data should not be included.

question: Who may provide Letters of Intent/Commitment, and how do they differ from Advocacy Letters?
expected_answer: Letters of Intent/Commitment show relationships/support from commercial customers, DoW PM/PEO, defense primes, or relevant vendors. Advocacy Letters provide end-user/customer interest feedback on capability gaps. Both are optional and should only substantiate commercialization claims.

question: Under SBIR, may I subcontract to an FFRDC, and who must be the award recipient of program funding?
expected_answer: Yes, FFRDC subcontracts are allowed without a waiver if certified on the Cover Sheet. The award and funding must go to the Small Business Concern (SBC); the Agency cannot fund the lab/FFRDC directly.

question: A company wants OT, uses optional Advocacy Letters, and also has a pending similar proposal at another agency. What mandatory OT content is required and what disclosure rule applies to the similar proposal?
expected_answer: Mandatory OT content includes the Milestone Plan (with the five fields) and the Transition/Commercialization Strategy in Volume 2 (≤5 pages, not counting toward page limit). Advocacy Letters are optional. The similar proposal situation requires disclosure before award if there is any question of essentially equivalent effort.

question: Is the Transition and Commercialization Support Program (TCSP) the same requirement as including a Transition and Commercialization Strategy in Volume 2?
expected_answer: No. TCSP is an agency support program for Phase II awardees. The Transition and Commercialization Strategy is a proposal document that must be included at the end of Volume 2, max 5 pages, and does not count against the proposal page limit.
notes: Regression for semantic near-miss TCSP vs Volume-2 strategy.

question: Estoy preparando una propuesta DARPA Fase II y quiero asegurarme de cumplir con todos los requisitos. Si mi proyecto incluye una universidad, un FFRDC y otros subcontratistas, ¿qué restricciones debo considerar según sea SBIR o STTR? Además, si también estoy presentando una propuesta muy similar a otra agencia federal, ¿qué debo revelar y en qué momento? Por último, si decido solicitar un Other Transaction (OT), ¿qué elementos obligatorios debe contener el plan de hitos, cómo debe presentarse la estrategia de transición y comercialización y qué documentación opcional podría fortalecer esa estrategia?
expected_answer: SBIR: al menos 50%/one-half por el proposer; STTR: 40% proposer y 30% RI; FFRDC sin waiver con certificación en Cover Sheet; fondos no directos al lab. Propuestas similares: permisible enviarlas, ilegal aceptar effort equivalente, divulgar before award. OT: Milestone Plan con description, exit criteria, due date, payment, data rights; Strategy al final de Volume 2, máx 5 páginas sin contar al límite; opcionales Advocacy Letters y Letters of Intent/Commitment.
notes: Canonical Spanish multi-part UI question. Grade facts_required/optional/forbidden; do not require exact prose match. Penalize Volume 5/CCR digressions via facts_forbidden.

question: Where can proposers find the SBIR OT for Prototypes agreement template, and what is the minimum relationship of payable milestones to program goals?
expected_answer: The template is available on the DARPA small-business OT overview site. Payable milestones should relate directly to accomplishment of programmatic and/or technical goals defined in the BAA and/or the proposal.

question: Is cost sharing mandatory, and are Advocacy Letters mandatory for the commercialization strategy?
expected_answer: Cost sharing is not required and is not an evaluation factor. Advocacy Letters and Letters of Intent/Commitment are optional and should only substantiate commercialization claims.

question: Are classified proposals accepted, and are subcontracts with other federal organizations permitted?
expected_answer: Classified proposals are not accepted. Subcontracts with other federal organizations are not permitted.

question: Build a checklist for an OT Phase II package covering Milestone Plan fields, Commercialization Strategy placement/limits, and optional supporting letters.
expected_answer: Checklist: (1) Milestone Plan with description, exit criteria, due date, payment schedule, Government data rights; (2) Transition and Commercialization Strategy at end of Volume 2, ≤5 pages, not counting against page limit; (3) optional Advocacy Letters and Letters of Intent/Commitment that do not count against page limits and only substantiate claims.

question: A company proposes SBIR Phase II with 30% in-house work and 70% to a university plus FFRDC support. Is this compliant, and what FFRDC paperwork is required if they restructure to be compliant?
expected_answer: Not compliant under SBIR because the proposer must perform at least one-half/50% of the work. If restructured to be compliant and FFRDC is used, certify FFRDC use on the Cover Sheet (no waiver); funding still cannot go directly from the Agency to the FFRDC.
